source_document: Virginia HB 2154 (High-Risk Artificial Intelligence Developer Duty of Care)
passage_text: A deployer of a high-risk artificial intelligence system is directed to complete an impact assessment for any high-risk artificial intelligence system on an annual basis and within ninety days following any intentional and substantial modification to the high-risk artificial intelligence system. The impact assessment should include an analysis of the reasonably foreseeable risks of algorithmic discrimination, a description of the categories of data processed, and the outputs produced by the system. This section does not preempt or supersede any obligation imposed under federal law.

obligation.subject: A deployer of a high-risk artificial intelligence system
obligation.action: complete an impact assessment for any high-risk artificial intelligence system

timeline.phase_in_period: annually and within ninety days following any intentional and substantial modification
timeline.timeline_text: on an annual basis and within ninety days following any intentional and substantial modification to the high-risk artificial intelligence system

preemption_signals: This section does not preempt or supersede any obligation imposed under federal law.

ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The threshold for what constitutes a 'substantial modification' triggering a new impact assessment is undefined. Routine model retraining, hyperparameter tuning, and data updates could all arguably be 'modifications' but their substantiality is subjective.
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Define quantitative thresholds for substantial modification, such as changes affecting more than a specified percentage of model parameters, training data, or output distributions

compliance_mechanism.description: Impact assessment including analysis of algorithmic discrimination risks, categories of data processed, and system outputs
compliance_mechanism.responsible_party: deployer of a high-risk artificial intelligence system
compliance_mechanism.reporting_frequency: annual and within 90 days of substantial modification

expected_extractions.notes: Tests non-standard obligation phrasing: 'is directed to' instead of shall/must. Also tests preemption_signals extraction and compliance mechanism co-extraction.
